key_insight: A mesh-preserving self-attention surrogate can learn PFEM free-surface fluid dynamics on evolving geometries while staying scalable enough for engineering use.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster surrogate simulation of free-surface and non-Newtonian flows without losing the mesh structure needed for remeshing, stability, and stress reconstruction.
method: Self-attention operates on mesh nodes to capture long-range spatial interactions.
A linear attention variant reduces cost for larger problems.
The PFEM mesh discretization is retained so standard finite element operators can recover derived fields.
evidence_signal: The models were tested on 2D and 3D free-surface flow benchmarks with evolving geometries, varying material parameters, and non-Newtonian fluids, and they accurately reproduced transient dynamics and final configurations.
watch_item: Performance depends on training data that covers the range of geometries, remeshing patterns, and rheologies seen at deployment; out-of-distribution cases may still be challenging.
paper_title: Attention mechanism for scalable mesh-based neural surrogates of free-surface fluids
